title: Audit Logs
accessRestrictedMsg: You don't have permission to view audit logs.

help.title: Audit Logs
help.intro: Audit Logs show administrative events recorded by Arthur, including who performed an action, what changed, when it happened, and the request IP when available. Use this section to investigate unexpected changes and review security-sensitive activity.
help.body: Read the entries from newest to oldest, use the page controls for older results, and reveal IP addresses only when they are needed for an investigation. Arthur records supported application events automatically; this page is not a record of every upstream API call.
help.tipInvestigate: Investigate a change: Look at the Date/Time and Item columns to find when and what was changed.
help.tipWho: Find out who did something: The User column shows which account performed the action.
help.tipSecurity: Security incident response: The IP column shows where the request came from — useful for identifying unauthorised access.
help.tipCompliance: Compliance reviews: Share the log with auditors to demonstrate that configuration changes are tracked.
help.retention: Important: audit entries are kept in application memory for up to 30 days and are lost when the backend restarts. If you need durable compliance records, collect or forward them through your operational logging process.
help.successBody: Correlate Date/Time, User, Action, Entity, Item, and IP with the change you are investigating. The fields should identify one event consistently; then compare it with the affected configuration or server Activity.
help.troubleshootingMissing: A missing event may be older than the in-memory retention window, removed by a backend restart, on another page, or not one of the events Arthur currently audits.
help.troubleshootingAccess: If the page is restricted, ask an administrator for the Audit Logs view permission. Audit permissions control access to all entries, not individual rows.
help.troubleshootingIp: A blank IP means the request did not provide a usable address. Behind a proxy, confirm that trusted forwarding headers are configured correctly before relying on the value.
help.dateTimeHelp: Shows when Arthur recorded the event, displayed in your browser's local timezone. Entries are listed newest first; use pagination to inspect older events.
help.dateTimeDetail: Compare this value with your incident timeline and the affected server's Activity. If times appear shifted, check the browser or operating-system timezone; the displayed value may differ from UTC timestamps in infrastructure logs.
help.userHelp: Shows the authenticated username associated with the event. Use it with Action and Item to identify which account made a specific change.
help.userDetail: Confirm that the account and action match an approved change. If the username is unexpected, review nearby events and the IP address; shared accounts make attribution less reliable.
help.actionHelp: The type of operation that was performed. Possible values:
help.actionCreated: Created (green) — A new object was added to the system.
help.actionUpdated: Updated (yellow) — An existing object was modified.
help.actionDeleted: Deleted (red) — An object was permanently removed.
help.actionGeneratedKey: Generated key (grey) — An MCP API key was created for a server.
help.actionRevokedKey: Revoked key (red) — An MCP API key was deleted, revoking all client access.
help.actionLogin: Login (grey) — A user successfully signed in.
help.actionDetail: Use the action to decide what to verify next: confirm created or updated records in their section, and treat deleted or revoked items as no longer available. Chip colours help scanning but do not indicate event severity.
help.entityHelp: The type of object that was affected by the action. Possible values: Server, Tool, User, Settings, API Key.
help.entityDetail: Combine Entity with Action to interpret the event, then confirm the affected area still reflects the expected state. Entity names describe the audited object type and may not match every navigation label word for word.
help.itemHelp: Shows the name or unique identifier of the specific record affected. Use it to distinguish events that share the same Action and Entity.
help.itemDetail: When no readable name was captured, Arthur shows an internal ID or a dash. Match an ID against related records or nearby events; a missing name does not by itself mean that the event failed.
